name: Santibáñez el Alto and Embalse
name_original: Pozuelo de Zarzón desde Guijo de Galisteo – circular por Santibáñez el Alto y el Embalse de Borbollón
description: This moderate 38.1-mile road cycling route takes you through charming villages and offers scenic views of the Embalse de Borbollón.
region_intro: Road cycling around Santibáñez El Alto offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, rural roads, and views of the surrounding reservoirs and agricultural areas. The region provides a mix of terrain suitable for various road cycling preferences, from gentle ascents to more challenging climbs. Cyclists can expect routes that traverse through small villages and natural settings, providing a tranquil experience. Best road cycling routes around Santibáñez El Alto The most popular road cycling route is Santibáñez el Alto and Embalse, a 38.1 miles (61.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete, offering scenic views of the reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Viewpoint of Hoyos – Navasfrías Town Center loop from Barrio El Camping-La Rivera, a difficult 54.9 miles (88.4 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and passes through various viewpoints and town centers. Local road cyclists also love the Robledillo de Gata – Cathedral of Santa María loop from Descargamaría, a 58.4 miles (93.9 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and historical points, often completed in about 4 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Santibáñez El Alto is defined by rural roads, rolling hills, and reservoir views. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santibáñez El Alto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Santibáñez El Alto?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes documented on komoot for the Santibáñez El Alto region. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and challenging options, allowing cyclists to explore the diverse landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Santibáñez El Alto?
A: The region is characterized by rolling hills, rural roads, and scenic views of reservoirs and agricultural areas. Cyclists will encounter a mix of terrain, from gentle ascents to more demanding climbs, providing a tranquil experience through small villages and natural settings.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Santibáñez El Alto?
A: Yes, there are a few routes suitable for beginners. The region offers 2 easy-rated routes, perfect for those looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and shorter distances.

Q: Can I find challenging long-distance road cycling routes in this area?
A: Absolutely. Santibáñez El Alto offers several challenging long-distance routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Hoyos – Cruz de Piedra de Villamiel loop from Rivera de Gata&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 70 km route with significant elevation gain, ideal for experienced cyclists seeking a demanding ride.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Santibáñez El Alto?
A: Many of the routes in the area are designed as loops, offering a convenient circular experience. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34334383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Stone Cross – Torre de Don Miguel loop from Torre de Don Miguel&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 31 km route that starts and ends in the same location.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can visit along the road cycling routes?
A: The region boasts several scenic spots and historical landmarks. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3333558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borbollón Dam&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4821468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Almenara&lt;/a&gt;. For breathtaking vistas, consider routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6968398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de los Ángeles Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural pools or waterfalls accessible from the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, you can find natural pools and even a waterfall. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrituelo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural attraction. Additionally, you might encounter natural pools like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4840158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riofrío Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Pool of Descargamaría&lt;/a&gt;, perfect for a refreshing stop.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santibáñez El Alto?
A: The region generally offers pleasant conditions for road cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons can still be enjoyable. Winters are typically cooler, but clear days can still provide good riding opportunities.

Q: Are there any less crowded or 'hidden gem' routes for road cyclists?
A: While popular routes like 'Santibáñez el Alto and Embalse' are well-loved, the region has 40 routes in total, many of which offer a quieter experience away from the main trails. Exploring some of the moderate or difficult routes, especially on weekdays, can lead to a more secluded ride through the rural landscapes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Santibáñez El Alto?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the diverse landscape, characterized by rolling hills and tranquil rural roads. The scenic views of the reservoirs and the opportunity to pass through charming small villages are often highlighted as key attractions, providing a peaceful and immersive cycling experience.

Q: Can I find places to eat or rest along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes traverse through or near small villages and town centers, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5690813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villasbuenas de Gata Village Square&lt;/a&gt;. These locations often provide opportunities for refreshments, cafes, or places to rest, allowing cyclists to refuel and take a break during their ride.
tips: Guijo de Galisteo is a small agricultural town near Montehermoso in the Alagón region.

Pozuelo de Zarzón is a small town in the Valle del Alagón region. The most outstanding monuments in the town are the 16th century San Pedro Parish Church and the six-cross granite Calvaries that welcome travelers from Guijo de Coria.

Go up to the town of Santibáñez el Alto and you will be delighted with spectacular views of the valley and the Borbollón Reservoir. A resounding defensive castle dominates the trade route through the Sierra de Gata.
Built in 1954, the Borbollón Reservoir was built to collect water from the Árrago River to use its waters to generate electricity and provide irrigation.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route conveniently starts in Guijo de Galisteo. You'll find parking available right at the starting point, making it easy to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly well-paved, consisting of about 52.3 km of asphalt and 9.04 km of other paved surfaces. Only a very small segment, less than 100 meters, is classified as a path. It's generally easy to ride for road cyclists.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is estimated to take approximately 2 hours and 53 minutes to complete, though your actual time may vary depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Due to its length of 61.3 kilometers and an elevation gain of about 690 meters, this route is categorized as moderate and requires good fitness. It might be challenging for absolute beginners, but experienced road cyclists will find it rewarding.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic views along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several highlights, including the charming Guijo de Galisteo Village Center, the Parish Church of San Pedro and Calvaries of Pozuelo de Zarzón, and the impressive Santibáñez el Alto Castle. A significant highlight is the View of Embalse de Borbollón, offering spectacular vistas of the reservoir and surrounding valley.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Cáceres province, where this route is located, can get very hot in summer. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling in Extremadura, providing comfortable conditions to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through villages like Guijo de Galisteo, Pozuelo de Zarzón, and Santibáñez el Alto. These towns typically offer local cafes or small establishments where you can stop for refreshments.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for cycling with dogs?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, primarily on paved surfaces. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, cycling with a dog on a leash alongside a road can be risky. It's best to consider the safety of your pet and other road users before bringing them on this specific type of route.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route or the Sierra de Gata area?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the general Sierra de Gata area through which it passes. You are free to enjoy the public roads and natural landscapes.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through very small sections of the Peña Gorda trail.

faqs.question: What makes cycling in the Sierra de Gata region unique?
faqs.answer: Cycling in the Sierra de Gata region offers a unique blend of varied landscapes, from river valleys to historical paths, and charming villages. This route, in particular, provides an excellent way to explore the region's natural beauty and cultural heritage, including views of the Embalse de Borbollón and historical sites.

waypoints.name: Parish Church of San Pedro and Calvaries of Pozuelo de Zarzón
